title: crataegus coccinea var. subvillosa
intro: Crataegus coccinea var. subvillosa is a variety of the Scarlet Hawthorn plant, a type of flowering shrub or small tree native to eastern North America. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, the plant's berries, leaves, and flowers are rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other compounds with potential health benefits. The medicinal properties of Crataegus coccinea var. subvillosa include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardiovascular-protective effects, which may contribute to its potential health benefits, such as lowering blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and reducing the risk of certain diseases. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and optimal dosing.
